title: Pipeline pressure test
subtitle: What fails at each stage, what the audit catches, what a human would do
intro: Twelve cards, one per pipeline node. Click to expand. Each card is keyed to evidence in research.md and to the spec's exit gates.

cards.name: Synthesize
cards.failure: Generator simulates 6 planted themes but exhibits them too literally — the 'latent' themes get named in the text instead of shown. The pipeline recovers 6/6 themes with a perfect score, and the number tells us nothing about real-world RTA.
cards.audit: Canary test that 'groundtruth/' is never present in any prompt. Inspect synthesized transcripts for analytic vocabulary (theme names, code labels).
cards.human: Treat synthetic recovery as a necessary but not sufficient gate. Run on at least one real corpus before trusting any number.

cards.name: StructureDoc
cards.failure: LLM proposes sections whose char_start..char_end does not tile the transcript (gaps or overlaps). Downstream: every 'section k of n' position becomes nonsense, and the coder prompt loses its anchor.
cards.audit: String-verify that concatenating all sections in order reproduces the transcript exactly, with no gap and no overlap. Lint gists for analytic vocabulary (no 'theme', 'code', 'pattern', 'meaning').
cards.human: A non-coding familiarization step — read the StructureDoc like a table of contents. Reject any section that summarizes the section rather than describes it.

cards.name: Segment
cards.failure: Excerpts lose their section context. The coder receives a floating paragraph with no position and no gist, and has to guess what the section is about from the excerpt alone.
cards.audit: Every Excerpt carries a non-null section_id. Every (k of n) tuple is valid for its section's length.
cards.human: Read a sample of excerpts. If you can't tell what part of the interview they came from without re-reading, the position metadata is broken.

cards.name: Code (N personas)
cards.failure: (1) Hallucinated quote: coder paraphrases the excerpt and the verbatim span string-match fails. (2) Latent code treated as a restatement of the semantic code — no interpretive lift. (3) Two personas produce identical codes, defeating the divergence RTA values.
cards.audit: Exact-match span gate before any code enters the codebook. Per-persona unique-code yield logged in the ledger — a persona that contributes 0 unique codes is dead weight.
cards.human: Sample 5–10 codes per persona. Ask: 'If I removed this code, would I lose a reading?' If no, the persona is collapsed to consensus.

cards.name: Verify span
cards.failure: Span passes string-match but is off by one word, or includes a speaker tag that wasn't in the original. String-match is necessary, not sufficient.
cards.audit: Span starts/ends at word boundaries (no partial words). Span does not include 'PHILLIPS:' / speaker tags that belong to the next turn.
cards.human: Click 10 random quotes in the report. Are they words the person actually said in that order?

cards.name: Reconcile
cards.failure: Drift memos are written but ignored. A section is coded against the arc of a different section, but no checkpoint surfaces this until the themes step.
cards.audit: Every reconcile step writes a Memo (even if 'no drift'). Memos are surfaced at the codebook checkpoint, not buried.
cards.human: Read the drift memos before approving the codebook. The Memos are the only signal that the structure pre-frame misled a coder.

cards.name: Conciliator merge
cards.failure: Conciliator merges two genuinely distinct codes because they share a word, or fails to merge two paraphrases of the same code because of surface vocabulary. Either way, the codebook stops being a faithful map of the data.
cards.audit: Every merge decision includes a written rationale. Minority (unmerged) codes are flagged, never deleted. The ratio of merge to create is itself a signal — too much merge = lossiness; too little = noise.
cards.human: Sample 20 merge decisions. Would you have made the same call? If the rationale sounds like a paraphrase of the codes rather than a judgment, the conciliator is broken.

cards.name: Checkpoint: codebook
cards.failure: Checkpoint is skipped via --auto. The human never sees the codebook, and the theme prompt inherits whatever biases the conciliator introduced.
cards.audit: An AutoDecision is written for every skipped checkpoint. The next step (themes) sees a marker that no human reviewed.
cards.human: Treat the codebook checkpoint as non-skippable in v0. Themes from an un-reviewed codebook are uninterpretable.

cards.name: Themes
cards.failure: Theorist produces cluster labels (e.g. 'food and farming') instead of central-concept statements. Themes become buckets, the failure mode Braun & Clarke warn against.
cards.audit: Structural validator: each theme has a central_concept_statement that is a claim, not a category. Validator checks length, tense, and that the statement could be falsified by data.
cards.human: Read the central-concept statement out loud. If it could be a slide title, it's a bucket. If it could be a thesis sentence, it's a theme.

cards.name: Checkpoint: themes
cards.failure: Human approves themes without checking the code-to-theme mapping. A theme with three codes is silently dropped because the report only displays the strongest six.
cards.audit: Theme → codes → excerpts → char spans must resolve. No orphans. The report's appendix shows every theme, not just the headline ones.
cards.human: Trace one theme end-to-end: definition → codes → excerpt → char span in the original transcript. If the trace breaks, the theme is not grounded.

cards.name: Recover (judge)
cards.failure: Judge is the same model family as the coder → self-preference bias (GPT-4 favors itself by ~10%). Judge is given candidate and planted themes in a fixed order → position bias (~10%). Recovery rate is reported without the synthetic_only label.
cards.audit: Judge is a different provider/family than the coder (env: MASSHINE_MODEL_JUDGE). Order is randomized per pair. Recovery rate is always labeled synthetic_only.
cards.human: Do not optimize for the recovery number. Optimize for the failure modes the recovery run exposes.

cards.name: Audit + report
cards.failure: Audit is run on the same machine state that produced the artifacts. If the codebook is corrupt, the audit confirms it.
cards.audit: Audit reads from disk only — never from in-memory state. The run directory is git-committed before audit runs.
cards.human: Treat audit exit-code 1 as a hard stop. A green report with a red audit is worse than a red report with a green audit.
